methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Neuromeka is a public robotics and manufacturing company listed on KOSDAQ (ticker 277810.KQ) that has raised $36.5M to date. The valuation assessment draws from a fragmented comparable universe: one public peer and eight private robotics firms including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics. The sector contains 11 same-sector peers, though concentration at different stages and scales limits direct comparability.

Peer-median methodology was not applicable due to insufficient narrow peer cohort. No same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier peers yielded the minimum three datapoints required for reliable triangulation. An all-sector median would introduce unacceptable distortion given the heterogeneity in robotics business models, capital intensity, and commercialization timelines across hardware, software, and hybrid offerings.

The analysis is materially constrained by limited public market data (one comparable) and the divergent profiles of private peers spanning autonomous systems, logistics automation, and manufacturing robotics. Without sufficient depth in any single valuation method, a defensible range cannot be derived from the provided dataset. Further diligence would require either expanded peer financials (revenue, margin profiles, growth rates) or transaction comps from the private market to establish meaningful benchmarks. The $36.5M raised provides a floor on invested capital but does not establish fair value absent additional market signals.
